sinners.name: Yi Sang

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to the author Yi Sang and his masterpiece 'The Wings'.

sinners.loreSummary: Yi Sang is a poet and intellectual whose work explores the fragmentation of the self.

sinners.name: Faust

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Goethe's Faust.

sinners.loreSummary: Faust is a polymath who knows all that can be known.

identities.displayName: The House of Spiders: The Ring Apprentice

egos.description: [HE] Ardor Blossom Star ÔÇö Gacha, Wrath affinity

cantos.summary: Introduced as the bus crew's calmest and most informed member, establishing her role as Dante's most unsettling source of answers.
Acts as a detached adviser through the K Corp incident, reinforcing how much she knows about the City while revealing little about herself.
The WARP Train incident exposes unusual vulnerability when her access to the Gesellschaft is disrupted, without making the story a dedicated Faust canto.
Remains one of the crew's most composed strategists during Hong Lu's family conflict, continuing the pattern of influence without a primary focus canto.

sinners.name: Don Quixote

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Cervantes' Don Quixote.
Informs the vampiric elements of her backstory and the La Manchaland kindred lore.

sinners.loreSummary: Don Quixote is a self-proclaimed knight errant on a quest for justice.

egos.displayName: In the Name of Love and Hate

cantos.summary: Impulsive desire to protect others causes unnecessary conflict during the LCB's attempt to enter K Corp.'s Nest.
One of her '863 dreams' fulfilled ÔÇö sailing the Great Lake in U Corp.'s District.
Focus character ÔÇö true identity as Sancho revealed; backstory as former Bloodfiend of La Manchaland explored; reconciles Sancho self with Don Quixote persona.
Current focus ÔÇö Don Quixote's role in the current seasonal transition.

sinners.name: Ryōshū

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Akutagawa's Hell Screen.
Referenced via the 'House of Spiders' and Ryoshu's complex relationship with her daughter and redemption.
Informs her cynical worldview on morality and survival within the City.
Reflects the aesthetic obsession and the weight of artistic sacrifice.

sinners.loreSummary: Ryōshū is an artist of violence who seeks to paint the ultimate scene.

cantos.summary: Present ÔÇö described as mysterious and calm, sketching while others panic.
Accepts an EGO gift from Yi Sang; cryptic dialogue throughout.

sinners.name: Meursault

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Camus' The Stranger.
Reflects his acceptance of the absurd through repetitive tasks and unwavering compliance.

sinners.loreSummary: Meursault is a man of absolute logic and total emotional detachment.

egos.description: [ZAYIN] Chain of Others ÔÇö Base, Pride affinity

cantos.summary: Noted for reciting 'irrelevant emoticons' while reading an email aloud ÔÇö peculiar behavior observed by the team.
Mentions wandering 'in search of life's joys'; assists during the Ahab confrontation.
Weighs mission success against Heathcliff's safety ÔÇö ultimately chooses Heathcliff's survival over the Golden Bough.
Past explored: activities deemed 'right' and 'wrong' by society; time at N Corp; familiarity with Vespa Crabro.
Fails to report the sudden disappearance of Limbus Company's target.
Childhood described ÔÇö mother scolded him similarly to how Outis reprimands others.

sinners.name: Hong Lu

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Dream of the Red Chamber.
Reflected in the character designs of the Pinky syndicate members associated with his lineage.
Elements of his journey and his family's vast, surreal hierarchy parallel the celestial trials.

sinners.loreSummary: Hong Lu is a noble scion whose family wealth hides a decaying core.

identities.displayName: The House of Spiders: The Ring Nursefather

cantos.summary: Takes the role of Young-ji in the League of Nine Litt├®rateurs play; demonstrates emotional maturity and insight into Yi Sang's mental state.
Empathetic and delicate side further developed through interactions with other characters.
Character depth continues to build.
Focus character ÔÇö backstory and family explored.

sinners.name: Heathcliff

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Wuthering Heights.

sinners.loreSummary: Heathcliff is a man of raw fury and obsessive love.

identities.displayName: W Corp. L4 Cleanup Agent - CCA

cantos.summary: Focus character ÔÇö processes trauma surrounding Catherine; shifts from vengeful anger to purposeful remembrance; weapon inscription changes from 'REVENGE' to 'REmember'.
Opens up to Dante and Don Quixote about his situation.

sinners.name: Ishmael

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Moby-Dick.
Mirrored in Queequeg's role and Ishmael's struggle with agency and self-sacrifice.
Reflects the psychological burden of being the sole survivor and the curse of her past experiences on the sea.

sinners.loreSummary: Ishmael is the sole survivor of a tragic whaling expedition.

identities.displayName: The House of Spiders: The Middle Apprentice

egos.description: [ZAYIN] Hundred-Footed Death Maggot ÔÇö Battle Pass (paid), Gloom affinity

cantos.summary: Takes a harsh blow for Dante ÔÇö demonstrates willingness to protect colleagues despite prior complaints about management.
Falls into neurosis when forced back to the Great Lake waters where her crew died ÔÇö devotes herself ceaselessly to preparations.
Focus character ÔÇö propensity for frenzy, excessive anger, violence, instability, and obsession peaks as she confronts Captain Ahab.

sinners.name: Rodion

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Crime and Punishment.

sinners.loreSummary: Rodion is a woman haunted by a crime she committed for 'principled' reasons.

cantos.summary: Focus character ÔÇö backstory with the Yurodiviye and District 25 explored; struggles with guilt tied to her past with her friend Sonya and a tragedy in her hometown.

sinners.name: Dante

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Dante's Divine Comedy.

sinners.loreSummary: Dante is the manager of the Limbus Company bus.

cantos.summary: Dante's past personality revealed ÔÇö snarky and aggressive before wiping their own memory and replacing their head with a prosthetic clock.
Gregor's focus ÔÇö Dante manages the bus and guides the team through their first real mission.
Rodion's focus ÔÇö Dante observes Rodya's moral conflict with the Yurodiviye.
Sinclair's focus ÔÇö Dante's encouragement of Sinclair is delayed and awkward; a turning point in Dante's management style.
Yi Sang's focus ÔÇö Dante manages the team through the dungeon and observes Meursault's peculiar behavior.
Ishmael's focus ÔÇö Dante refines leadership as Ishmael's frenzy arc peaks and she confronts Captain Ahab.
Heathcliff's focus ÔÇö Dante observes Heathcliff choose love over mission objective.
Don Quixote's focus ÔÇö Dante uses the Superbia ability to strengthen Don Quixote; clock hands advance.
Hong Lu's focus ÔÇö Dante participates in the Xichun rescue plan.
Gregor's past explored ÔÇö Dante gains Remote Sight ability and explores the Malthael ability.
Current event ÔÇö Details pending lore progression.

powers.name: Turning Back the Clock
powers.description: Dante can heal injuries and revive Sinners from death by winding back time. The cost is that Dante physically feels how each Sinner died — the pain writhes through their body as time reverses. Dante describes opening a foreboding door filled with the wails of unknown sufferers and pulling the Sinners' hands out from the gateway.

powers.name: Golden Bough Resonance
powers.description: Dante can sense where the Golden Boughs are by "following their star." They perceive the Boughs as shining stars in a swirling sky and can share these visions with the Sinners through their telepathic link.

powers.name: Telepathy
powers.description: Dante communicates only through a ticking sound, altering its frequency and intensity. Only the Sinners and a select few can understand the meaning behind the ticks. When agitated, the speech may include bell chimes and/or steam whistles. Dante can selectively address one Sinner or the entire group at once.

powers.name: Distortion Speech
powers.description: Dante can understand Distortions who have lost the ability to speak human language, extending to their subordinate entities. Acting as interpreter, they relay invaluable information to the Sinners — possibly due to resonance with the Golden Boughs.

powers.name: Remote Sight
powers.description: Gained during the events of Canto IX. Dante can watch over Sinners separated from them, though cannot verbally communicate. They can also "lend strength" and revive distant Sinners.

powers.name: Sapling of Light: Pigritia
powers.description: In Canto VI, Dante creates a field that slows everything except the Sinners within a certain radius. In game, this halves the Speed of all enemies, rounded down.

powers.name: Sapling of Light: Superbia
powers.description: In Canto VII, Dante appears to strengthen the power of a Sinner. In game, this allows an E.G.O. Skill to be slotted without spending any E.G.O Resources.

powers.name: Sapling of Light: Morositas
powers.description: In Canto VIII, Dante uses the strength-averaging aspect of their contract to bring an enemy down to the Sinners' level. In game, this sets the Offense and Defense levels of all units to the average of all units' values for three turns.

powers.name: Sapling of Light: Ira
powers.description: In Canto IX, Dante uses the strength-enhancing aspect of their contract to drastically raise a Sinner's capabilities for the duration of the fight. In game: unbreakable coins, no stagger, minimum HP fixed to 10, and base attacks heal HP. All effects last one turn.

powers.name: Last Resort
powers.description: A preventative measure to prevent the Golden Boughs from falling into wrong hands. By inputting a sequence into the back of their clock head, Dante can self-detonate a charge hot enough to "melt even the Stars of the City." The Golden Boughs cannot survive this explosion.

powers.name: Golden Bough Synchronization
powers.description: Used once during Canto 6-48 to perform an otherwise impossible feat with the help of a Golden Bough, leaving it "burnt" afterwards.

powers.name: Immunity to Memory Wiping
powers.description: Dante can resist memory removal from various sources. They retained their memory of K Corp.'s Singularity after the wipe at the end of Canto IV, and retain memories of characters erased from existence, such as Catherine (Canto VI) and Ren and Sora (Relic Arayashiki effect).

sinners.name: Sinclair

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Hesse's Demian.
Reflected in Sinclair's 'Mark', signifying the burden of awareness and the exclusion from the world of innocence.
Demian quotes the Fox; Sinclair's design (scarf) and themes of lost innocence mirror the Prince.
His journey of self-discovery mirrors Siddhartha's search for enlightenment outside traditional upbringing.

sinners.loreSummary: Sinclair is a young man struggling with the darkness within himself.

cantos.summary: Focus character ÔÇö demonstrates rougher side capable of grudges, violence, and disdain; confronts Kromer and develops self-confidence.
Empathy particularly prominent ÔÇö assists Ishmael during the Ahab confrontation.

sinners.name: Outis

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Homer's Odyssey.

sinners.loreSummary: Outis is a tactical expert who hides her true self behind a mask of duty.

egos.displayName: I'll Go fer Scissors. How 'Bout You?

cantos.summary: Dungeon play scenario involving Shrenne.
References years of wandering before joining Limbus Company.
Debates Don Quixote's nature with other Sinners.
Expresses apprehension about Xichun's rescue plan.

sinners.name: Gregor

literarySources.specificConnection: Direct mapping to Kafka's The Metamorphosis.
Gregor's relationship with authority (Hermann/Manager) mirrors Kafka's real-life struggle with his father.

sinners.loreSummary: Gregor is a man transformed by an experimental procedure into something insectoid.

cantos.summary: Focus character ÔÇö background revealed; insectile bio-prosthetic arm; complicated relationship with mother Hermann.
Central figure ÔÇö undergoes 'Roach Emperor' transformation; past with Hermann comes to the forefront.
